title: Figure 3 in Extravisual opsins in the blind shrimp Creaseria morleyi: presence and expression
author: Malpica-Calderón Alejandro Botello, José R. Pérez-Calderón Jorge A. Pérez-León Nuno Simões Marisela Aguirre-Ramírez Roxana E.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 3.Electrophoresis of cDNA of amplified long wavelength opsin products by PCR from cDNA obtained by RT-PCR performed on RNA samples of Creaseria morleyi. cDNA from the sixth abdominal segment plus uropods (6A+U), abdomen (A), eyes (E), and cephalothorax (C), showed PCR products using specific primers for long wavelength opsin 1. The smallest band shown from the DNA ladder (L) is 500 bp, a 100 bp distance separates each band between the band corresponding to 500 bp ant and the band of 1000 bp. The expected amplicon was of 848 pb and can be observed in eyes and abdomen in continuous light condition, and in abdomen and in sixth abdominal segment plus uropods in total darkness condition.

The smallest band shown from the DNA ladder (L) is 500 bp, a 100 bp distance separates each band between the band corresponding to 500 bp ant and the band of 1000 bp. The expected amplicon was of 848 pb and can be observed in eyes and abdomen in continuous light condition, and in abdomen and in sixth abdominal segment plus uropods in total darkness condition.
